source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

Come own how enterprise customers adopt, integrate, and scale Neon’s AI automation platform. You’ll lead technical implementations, design and improve customer workflows, diagnose root-cause issues, and translate requirements between customers and engineering. Ideal candidates bring strong analytical and technical problem-solving skills, thrive in ambiguity, and have experience operating in hybrid product + customer-facing roles.





ABOUT US: JOIN THE TEAM MAKING LIFE-SAVING DRUGS ACCESSIBLE

The crisis: Sarah delayed cancer treatment for weeks facing $30,000 monthly costs. Marcus's autoimmune condition worsened while battling insurance denials.

Over 50% of critical prescriptions are abandoned due to:

 - Cost barriers: Patients with high copays are 5x more likely to abandon treatment

 - Insurance maze: Complex prior authorizations block access

 - Logistical challenges: Coordinating medical visits for treatments

Our solution: Neon's technology eliminates these obstacles by automating patient access workflows for pharma companies: —automating authorizations, streamlining benefits verification, and unlocking financial assistance.

Join our mission: We want to live in a world where every patient can navigate the healthcare system with ease, especially when it matters most. Help us ensure that access to medicine is determined by clinical need—not bureaucracy or financial constraint. When Neon succeeds, patients access life-saving treatments without bankrupting their families, while we build an AI automation powerhouse serving healthcare's biggest enterprises.





WHY JOIN NEON?

 - Frighteningly ambitious: We’re not just idealists. We’re seasoned builders. On a mission to build a $200B+ company—on the scale of Palantir or ServiceNow—serving the largest healthcare enterprises.

 - Experienced founding team: Built by exited founders, YC & MIT alum, ex-Tesla, ex-Google engineers.

 - Hypergrowth with stability: We went from initial idea to 7+ figure customer contracts in just 4 months—in an industry where sales cycles typically take 12-18 months. We are profitable and relentlessly focused on execution.

 - Powerhouse backing: We’re funded by elite Silicon Valley VCs who've backed unicorns like DoorDash, Lyft, and Mammoth Biosciences. And strategic healthcare investors with deep industry connections.

 - Outsized impact & opportunity: Work at the intersection of agentic AI, healthcare transformation, and life-changing patient outcomes.

 - Career acceleration: Join early and grow rapidly with us as we scale toward category dominance in healthcare automation.


WHAT’S UNIQUE ABOUT WORKING HERE?

Mission-driven capitalists

We’re a rare blend of mission-driven capitalists. We are on the path towards building a $200B+ business while dramatically improving the healthcare system—and patients’ lives.

Working like athletes

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.




JOBS TO BE DONE


Partner directly with enterprise customers to ensure successful adoption and deep integration of Neon’s AI automation platform. Operate as the connective tissue between customer workflows, engineering, and product.

 - Own the relationship: Build trust and act as the primary point of contact for customer stakeholders, from operators to executives.

 - Translate customer needs into technical specs: Rigorously map workflows, translate needs into technical requirements, and proactively identify edge cases.

 - Drive implementations: Hand-hold customers through onboarding and integration.

 - Diagnose issues: Investigate edge cases, uncover root causes, and work with engineering to resolve system or workflow challenges.

 - Manage programs: Track timelines, risks, and dependencies across multiple complex initiatives.

 - Expand usage: Iden

source_jobs.title: Growth Specialist
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

You’ll be building the growth engine that fuels Neon’s go-to-market motion. You will craft sharp content, spin up demand systems, run outbound campaigns, and architect experiments that unlock new channels. This is a role for self-directed builders who thrive at the intersection of writing, digital marketing, and automation, comfortable juggling content, events, outbound sequencing, and growth operations to drive pipeline and accelerate revenue.

THE ROLE: GROWTH SPECIALIST

Build Neon’s go-to-market engine alongside the head of GTM and CEO. Prioritize ruthlessly, self-direct, and chase leverage wherever it lives.

Some examples of what you might work on:

 - Create content that drives thought leadership and demand, crafting messages with clarity and hype.

 - Spin up growth systems: SEO, paid ads, retargeting, referral loops. Recruit and manage agencies/contractors for these functions as needed.

 - Plan and run industry events end-to-end, converting attendees into advocates and customers.

 - Develop sharp sales collateral: decks, one-pagers, positioning guides.

 - Architect outbound campaigns (Apollo, Outreach, etc.) targeting pharma decision-makers, testing messaging, and iterating fast.

 - Maintain outbound deliverability (domains, DMARC/DKIM).

 - Run rapid experiments to

source_jobs.title: AI Quality Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

Ensure Neon’s automation agents perform flawlessly in real-world workflows. You’ll monitor outputs, catch errors, refine processes, and feed insights back into product and engineering. Ideal candidates are detail-oriented, comfortable with technical systems, and thrive in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where quality is mission critical.




ABOUT US: JOIN THE TEAM MAKING LIFE-SAVING DRUGS ACCESSIBLE

The crisis: Sarah delayed cancer treatment for weeks facing $30,000 monthly costs. Marcus's autoimmune condition worsened while battling insurance denials.

Over 50% of critical prescriptions are abandoned due to:

 - Cost barriers: Patients with high copays are 5x more likely to abandon treatment

 - Insurance maze: Complex prior authorizations block access

 - Logistical challenges: Coordinating medical visits for treatments

Join our mission: We want to live in a world where every patient can navigate the healthcare system with ease, especially when it matters most. Help us ensure that access to medicine is determined by clinical need—not bureaucracy or financial constraint. When Neon succeeds, patients access life-saving treatments without bankrupting their families, while we build an AI automation powerhouse serving healthcare's biggest enterprises.




WHY JOIN NEON?

 - Frighteningly ambitious: We’re not just idealists. We’re seasoned builders. On a mission to build a $200B+ company—on the scale of Palantir or ServiceNow—serving the largest healthcare enterprises.

 - Experienced founding team: Built by exited founders, YC & MIT alum, ex-Tesla, ex-Google engineers.

 - Hypergrowth with stability: We went from initial idea to 7+ figure customer contracts in just 4 months—in an industry where sales cycles typically take 12-18 months. We are profitable and relentlessly focused on execution.

 - Powerhouse backing: We’re funded by elite Silicon Valley VCs who've backed unicorns like DoorDash, Lyft, and Mammoth Biosciences. And strategic healthcare investors with deep industry connections.

 - Outsized impact & opportunity: Work at the intersection of agentic AI, healthcare transformation, and life-changing patient outcomes.

 - Career acceleration: Join early and grow rapidly with us as we scale toward category dominance in healthcare automation.




WHAT’S UNIQUE ABOUT WORKING HERE?

Mission-driven capitalists

We’re a rare blend of mission-driven capitalists. We are on the path towards building a $200B+ business while dramatically improving the healthcare system—and patients’ lives.

Working like athletes

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.




THE ROLE: AI QUALITY OPERATOR

Improve the quality of our AI agents by reviewing the output of their work. For example, for our phone-calling AI agents, you will:

 - Review transcripts and recordings of their calls

 - Understand issues that arose on those calls

 - Label and document those issues

 - Provide suggested guidance on how to improve going forward

Your efforts will contribute directly to the quality of our AI agents, and thus to our mission.




DNA

Core traits and capabilities of a high-performing Neon quality operator.

 - Detail-oriented: You will need to maintain outstanding attention to detail, as you review AI agent work product at scale.

 - Speed: You will need to execute with speed and urgency.

 - Rigorous: You will need to often investigate and deeply understand a problem, in order to suggest helpful solutions. To that end, you must be willing to do the hard thinking required to understand the root cause of an issue

We’re a rare blend of mission-driven capitalists. We are on the path towards building a $200B+ business while dramatically improving the healthcare system—and patients’ lives.

Working like athletes

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.




SKILLS REQUIRED

Technical skills:

 - Proficiency with Node, Typescript, and Python.

 - Good understanding of system design concepts - distributed systems, reliability/performance tradeoffs

 - Experience in API design, implementation, and documentation

 - Experience using AI coding tools (Cursor, Github Copilot, Claude, etc)

 - Experience working with CI/CD systems and good DevOps skills a nice to have

 - Excellent documentation and testing habits

 - Proficiency with version control (Git)

Soft skills:

 - 10x mindset - you’ll be among the first engineering hires!

 - You are a fun human!

 - Good time management and planning skills




EXPERIENCE

4-7 years of experience (or equivalent) as a software engineer.

We’re leveling this role to Pave’s “P4” level https://support.pave.com/hc/en-us/articles/4430985339799-Job-Levels:

   - Define priorities
   
   - Suggest good solutions
   
   - Excel at implementation

 - You pick up concepts quickly, pattern match on past problems.

 - You are obsessed with speed and quality, and help embed that quality into the rest of the

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

We are hiring an Executive Assistant (EA) to partner with Neon’s leadership team and keep the company operating at high velocity. This role will manage critical scheduling, communications, and operational priorities so our executives can focus on scaling Neon. Ideal candidates are highly organized, thrive in ambiguity, and bring discretion, judgment, and startup hustle.




ABOUT US: JOIN THE TEAM MAKING LIFE-SAVING DRUGS ACCESSIBLE

 - Experienced founding team: Built by exited founders, YC & MIT alum, ex-Tesla, ex-Google engineers.

Working like athletes

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.




JOBS TO BE DONE

As an Executive Assistant, you will ensure our leadership team is operating at maximum leverage.

 - Calendar and scheduling: Manage complex calendars, travel, and logistics.

 - Information flow: Ensure executives are prepared with the right context for every meeting.

 - Operations support: Drive follow-ups, document notes, and manage critical tasks.

 - Event coordination: Plan leadership meetings, board meetings, and offsites.

 - Confidential projects: Handle sensitive initiatives with discretion and judgment.




EA DNA

Traits of a high-performing Neon Executive Assistant.

 - Hyper-organized: Masters complex scheduling and priorities.

 - Trusted partner: Exercises discretion and earns confidence from executives.

 - Proactive operator: Anticipates needs and solves problems before they surface.

 - Adaptable: Thrives in fast-moving, ambiguous environments.

 - Communicator: Clear, concise

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

We are hiring an Account Executive to drive new business and expand relationships with enterprise customers. This role is focused on selling Neon’s automation platform to healthcare and life sciences organizations, owning the full sales cycle from prospecting through close. Ideal candidates have enterprise SaaS sales experience, thrive in high-growth environments, and are motivated to build a category-defining company.





ABOUT US: JOIN THE TEAM MAKING LIFE-SAVING DRUGS ACCESSIBLE

The crisis: Sarah delayed cancer treatment for weeks facing $30,000 monthly costs. Marcus's autoimmune condition worsened while battling insurance denials.

Over 50% of critical prescriptions are abandoned due to:

 - Cost barriers: Patients with high copays are 5x more likely to abandon treatment

 - Insurance maze: Complex prior authorizations block access

 - Logistical challenges: Coordinating medical visits for treatments

Our solution: Neon's technology eliminates these obstacles by automating patient access workflows for pharma companies: —automating authorizations, streamlining benefits verification, and unlocking financial assistance.

Join our mission: We want to live in a world where every patient can navigate the healthcare system with ease, especially when it matters most. Help us ensure that access to medicine is determined by clinical need—not bureaucracy or financial constraint. When Neon succeeds, patients access life-saving treatments without bankrupting their families, while we build an AI automation powerhouse serving healthcare's biggest enterprises.





JOBS TO BE DONE

Neon's sales motion is highly-consultative and highly-technical.

 - As such, you should:
   
   - be computer-science-literate. i.e. You should be able to speak confidently with IT leaders at our clients' teams about our technical implementation details: APIs, schemas, high-level architecture, etc. If you have interfaced directly with engineering/IT teams before, this will probably suffice.
   
   - be comfortable doing deep discovery to understand the prospects' current workflows and challenges with those workflows.

Sales

 - Prospect and qualify leads: Identify and engage key decision-makers at enterprise healthcare organizations.

 - Run discovery and demos: Understand customer pain points and showcase Neon's automation platform.

 - Manage pipeline: Own opportunities end to end, from outreach through contract negotiation and close.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Work with marketing, product, and customer success to ensure customer needs are met.

 - Exceed revenue targets: Drive consistent quota attainment and contribute to company growth.

Implementation & Success

 - Beyond the initial sale, you will be involved in implementation and ongoing success: partnering directly with enterprise customers to ensure success with Neon’s automation platform. Act as the connective tissue between client stakeholders, our product/engineering teams, and leadership.

 - Own the relationship: Build trust and act as the primary point of contact for senior client stakeholders.

 - Drive implementations: Translate business needs into product rollouts, ensuring smooth deployment and adoption.

 - Shape new products: Take customer problems from design through implementation, coordinating cross-functional resources.

 - Manage programs: Track timelines, risks, and outcomes across multiple initiatives.

 - Identify expansion opportunities: Spot opportunities for deeper adoption and increased value.




TECHNICAL CONSULTATIVE DNA

Traits of a high-performing Neon Health AE

 - Strategic operator: Can think at the level of management consulting and execution.

 - Able to own complex deals: managing red-lines and complex contract negotiations.

 - Program management excellence: Skilled at structuring projects, driving clarity, and delivering on commitments.

 - Technical curiosity: Comfortable digging into product workflows and technical details.

 - Trusted advisor: B

source_jobs.title: VP of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

We are hiring a VP of Engineering to lead Neon’s engineering team and enable the CTO to focus on strategically scaling the company. This leader will own execution across engineering, build and mentor a world-class team, and ensure we continue shipping high-quality products at startup speed. The timeline to hire is by early 2026.




ABOUT US: JOIN THE TEAM MAKING LIFE-SAVING DRUGS ACCESSIBLE

The crisis: Sarah delayed cancer treatment for weeks facing $30,000 monthly costs. Marcus's autoimmune condition worsened while battling insurance denials.

Over 50% of critical prescriptions are abandoned due to:

 - Cost barriers: Patients with high copays are 5x more likely to abandon treatment

 - Insurance maze: Complex prior authorizations block access

 - Logistical challenges: Coordinating medical visits for treatments

Our solution: Neon's technology eliminates these obstacles by automating patient access workflows for pharma companies: —automating authorizations, streamlining benefits verification, and unlocking financial assistance.

Join our mission: We want to live in a world where every patient can navigate the healthcare system with ease, especially when it matters most. Help us ensure that access to medicine is determined by clinical need—not bureaucracy or financial constraint. When Neon succeeds, patients access life-saving treatments without bankrupting their families, while we build an AI automation powerhouse serving healthcare's biggest enterprises.




WHY JOIN NEON?

 - Frighteningly ambitious: We’re not just idealists. We’re seasoned builders. On a mission to build a $200B+ company—on the scale of Palantir or ServiceNow—serving the largest healthcare enterprises.

 - Experienced founding team: Built by exited founders, YC & MIT alum, ex-Tesla, ex-Google engineers.

 - Hypergrowth with stability: We went from initial idea to 7+ figure customer contracts in just 4 months—in an industry where sales cycles typically take 12-18 months. We are profitable and relentlessly focused on execution.

 - Powerhouse backing: We’re funded by elite Silicon Valley VCs who've backed unicorns like DoorDash, Lyft, and Mammoth Biosciences. And strategic healthcare investors with deep industry connections.

 - Outsized impact & opportunity: Work at the intersection of agentic AI, healthcare transformation, and life-changing patient outcomes.

 - Career acceleration: Join early and grow rapidly with us as we scale toward category dominance in healthcare automation.




WHAT’S UNIQUE ABOUT WORKING HERE?

Mission-driven capitalists

We’re a rare blend of mission-driven capitalists. We are on the path towards building a $200B+ business while dramatically improving the healthcare system—and patients’ lives.

Working like athletes

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.

Frequent offsites

We spend one week every month offsite in beautiful places like Tahoe, Squamish, Mendocino, the Santa Cruz mountains, and Monterrey. We ship an incredible amount of product on these offsites. And we have a blast — climbing, swimming, surfing, and otherwise enjoying these beautiful places.

Rediscover the magic of coding late into the night, in the zone. Side by side with a team that—through their example of excellence—is inspiring you to reach your potential.

It’s not for everyone.

But if you long for an intense camaraderie that you can’t find elsewhere, then Neon is the place for you.




JOBS TO BE DONE

Lead Neon’s engineering function to support rapid product innovation and scale.

 - Own engineering execution: Ensure products are shipped reliably, securely, and at startup velocity.

 - Build the team: Recruit, mentor, and retain exceptional engineers.

 - Develop processes: Create systems that balance speed with quality as we grow.

source_jobs.title: Growth Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

Work directly with the CEO on the highest impact initiatives. This role combines consulting-level strategy with startup execution, tackling whatever it takes to drive Neon’s growth. Ideal candidates bring top-tier consulting and/or MBA backgrounds, thrive in ambiguity, and are eager to operate as a hands-on builder shaping GTM and business strategy.





ABOUT US: JOIN THE TEAM MAKING LIFE-SAVING DRUGS ACCESSIBLE

The crisis: Sarah delayed cancer treatment for weeks facing $30,000 monthly costs. Marcus's autoimmune condition worsened while battling insurance denials.

Over 50% of critical prescriptions are abandoned due to:

 - Cost barriers: Patients with high copays are 5x more likely to abandon treatment

 - Insurance maze: Complex prior authorizations block access

 - Logistical challenges: Coordinating medical visits for treatments

Our solution: Neon's technology eliminates these obstacles by automating patient access workflows for pharma companies: —automating authorizations, streamlining benefits verification, and unlocking financial assistance.

Join our mission: We want to live in a world where every patient can navigate the healthcare system with ease, especially when it matters most. Help us ensure that access to medicine is determined by clinical need—not bureaucracy or financial constraint. When Neon succeeds, patients access life-saving treatments without bankrupting their families, while we build an AI automation powerhouse serving healthcare's biggest enterprises.





WHY JOIN NEON?

 - Frighteningly ambitious: We’re not just idealists. We’re seasoned builders. On a mission to build a $200B+ company—on the scale of Palantir or ServiceNow—serving the largest healthcare enterprises.

 - Experienced founding team: Built by exited founders, YC & MIT alum, ex-Tesla, ex-Google engineers.

 - Hypergrowth with stability: We went from initial idea to 7+ figure customer contracts in just 4 months—in an industry where sales cycles typically take 12-18 months. We are profitable and relentlessly focused on execution.

 - Powerhouse backing: We’re funded by elite Silicon Valley VCs who've backed unicorns like DoorDash, Lyft, and Mammoth Biosciences. And strategic healthcare investors with deep industry connections.

 - Outsized impact & opportunity: Work at the intersection of agentic AI, healthcare transformation, and life-changing patient outcomes.

 - Career acceleration: Join early and grow rapidly with us as we scale toward category dominance in healthcare automation.





WHAT’S UNIQUE ABOUT WORKING HERE?

Mission-driven capitalists

We’re a rare blend of mission-driven capitalists. We are on the path towards building a $200B+ business while dramatically improving the healthcare system—and patients’ lives.

Working like athletes

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.




JOBS TO BE DONE

Work as the right hand to the CEO to drive growth across the company. This role is not narrowly defined—it’s about doing whatever it takes to accelerate Neon’s trajectory.

 - Design and execute growth initiatives: From market strategy to hands-on campaigns.

 - Partner with leadership: Translate vision into operating plans, OKRs, and measurable outcomes.

 - Crack complex problems: Break down ambiguous challenges into structured projects and drive them to completion.

 - Own cross-functional projects: Coordinate product, GTM, and operations to unlock growth.

 - Identify leverage: Ruthlessly prioritize the highest-impact opportunities.




TRAITS OF A HIGH-PERFORMING GROWTH CHIEF OF STAFF

 - Consulting-caliber intensity: Exceptionally smart, driven, and able to thrive under pressure.

 - Analytical strategist: Exceptional problem solver with consulting-level rigor.

 - Operator mentality: Can alternate between s

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TLDR

You'll be hands-on in improving the real-world behavior of our AI systems — tracing and fixing runtime issues, building agent simulators, designing LLM evals and QA tools, and interfacing with client data. This is a role for builders who like prompt-level debugging, LLM system testing, and building infrastructure that improves our AI agents’ performance.

Like athletes, we are constantly honing our craft to produce our best possible work.

We work with intention and humility. We support and challenge each other to be our best selves.

And as a team, we achieve goals together that would be impossible alone.

Frequent offsites

We spend one week every month offsite in beautiful places like Tahoe, Squamish, Mendocino, the Santa Cruz mountains, and Monterrey. We ship an incredible amount of product on these offsites. And we have a blast — climbing, swimming, surfing, and otherwise enjoying these beautiful places.

Rediscover the magic of coding late into the night, in the zone. Side by side with a team that—through their example of excellence—is inspiring you to reach your potential.

It’s not for everyone.

But if you long for an intense camaraderie that you can’t find elsewhere, then Neon is the place for you.




THE ROLE: SOFTWARE ENGINEER


WHAT YOU’LL DO

You’ll work across our AI agent platform — writing prompts, debugging runtime issues, building agent simulation tooling, creating evals, interfacing with client data, and helping us monitor system behavior at scale.

notes: Neon Health is a product-heavy company with a strong focus on AI automation and patient access workflows. The company has a mix of technical and non-technical roles, with a high proportion of engineering and product positions.
